message:
Address PR review feedback

- Use `positional and named arguments` instead of `arguments and options`
- Simplify field type examples: use `hash` instead of `this.hash`
- Add note about type checking only applying to constant expressions
- Fix field type example values in tables

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@ Call this operator with positional arguments:
 
 ```tql
 from {x: 1}
-pkg::tag this.name, "Alice"
+pkg::tag name, "Alice"
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -136,7 +136,7 @@ Call this operator with both arguments and options:
 
 ```tql
 from {x: 1}
-pkg::tag this.name, "Alice", prefix="User: "
+pkg::tag name, "Alice", prefix="User: "
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -158,15 +158,15 @@ Each parameter supports the following fields:
 
 The `type` field constrains what values the parameter accepts:
 
-| Type     | Description                                                |
-| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
-| `field`  | A field selector (e.g., `this.name`). Cannot have defaults |
-| `string` | A string literal or expression                             |
-| `int`    | An integer value                                           |
-| `double` | A floating-point value                                     |
-| `bool`   | A boolean value                                            |
-| `ip`     | An IP address                                              |
-| `secret` | A secret string (accepts string literals)                  |
+| Type     | Description                                           |
+| -------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
+| `field`  | A field selector (e.g., `name`). Cannot have defaults |
+| `string` | A string literal or expression                        |
+| `int`    | An integer value                                      |
+| `double` | A floating-point value                                |
+| `bool`   | A boolean value                                       |
+| `ip`     | An IP address                                         |
+| `secret` | A secret string (accepts string literals)             |
 
 If you omit the `type` field, the parameter accepts any value.
 
@@ -225,7 +225,7 @@ $target = $target * 2
 
 ```tql
 from {count: 5, score: 10}
-pkg::double_value this.count
+pkg::double_value count
 ```
 
 ```tql

@@ -175,7 +175,7 @@ length.
 So far, our operators are static—they always do the same thing. But what if we
 want to make them more flexible? [Parameterized
 operators](/explanations/packages#parameterized-operators) allow you to pass
-arguments and options, just like built-in operators.
+positional and named arguments, just like built-in operators.
 
 Let's create a reusable operator that tags events with threat intelligence
 metadata. We'll make the confidence level configurable:
@@ -215,7 +215,7 @@ Now you can call this operator with different configurations:
 ```tql
 // Use defaults
 from {hash: "abc123"}
-sslbl::tag_threat this.hash
+sslbl::tag_threat hash
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -225,7 +225,7 @@ sslbl::tag_threat this.hash
 ```tql
 // Override confidence
 from {hash: "abc123"}
-sslbl::tag_threat this.hash, confidence="High"
+sslbl::tag_threat hash, confidence="High"
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -235,14 +235,14 @@ sslbl::tag_threat this.hash, confidence="High"
 ```tql
 // Override both options
 from {hash: "abc123"}
-sslbl::tag_threat this.hash, confidence="High", source="Custom Feed"
+sslbl::tag_threat hash, confidence="High", source="Custom Feed"
 ```
 
 ```tql
 {hash: "abc123", threat: {indicator: "abc123", confidence: "High", source: "Custom Feed"}}
 ```
 
-The `field` type is special—it accepts field selectors like `this.hash` and
+The `field` type is special—it accepts field selectors like `hash` and
 allows your operator to work with any field the caller specifies.
 
 #### Supported parameter types
@@ -251,15 +251,17 @@ You can constrain parameters to specific types:
 
 | Type     | Description                          | Example value |
 | -------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------- |
-| `field`  | A field selector                     | `this.name`   |
+| `field`  | A field selector                     | `field_name`  |
 | `string` | Text values                          | `"hello"`     |
 | `int`    | Integer numbers                      | `42`          |
 | `double` | Floating-point numbers               | `3.14`        |
 | `bool`   | Boolean values                       | `true`        |
 | `ip`     | IP addresses                         | `192.168.1.1` |
 | `secret` | Sensitive strings (handled securely) | `"api-key"`   |
 
-Omit the `type` field to accept any value. All types accept `null`.
+Omit the `type` field to accept any value. All types accept `null`. Type
+checking only applies to constant expressions; for runtime data, the type serves
+as documentation only.
 
 #### Test parameterized operators
 
@@ -268,7 +270,7 @@ test files that exercise different argument combinations:
 
 ```tql title="tests/tag_threat.tql"
 from {hash: "test123"}
-sslbl::tag_threat this.hash, confidence="High", source="Test"
+sslbl::tag_threat hash, confidence="High", source="Test"
 ```
 
 This ensures your operator handles various inputs correctly
